reviews.review_description: This game is scary I love it the atmosphere is creepy One thing Anthony party need to be fixed it's a jump scare when she pops up in just his go figure this goes in your face and shakes and screams she'll be something more terrifying I mutated woman or witch or something and she should jump scare you from different angles of the bed like on the side or in front of you or behind you or where is she appears at That's what she should scare you at either than that the game is good that just need to be changed the whole gold ghost shaking in your face it kind of ruins experience a little bit for me but it's good you can change that and make it more it's terrifying this would be something

developer_response.body:
Thanks for playing SleepWatch and for the detailed feedback!

I'm really glad you enjoyed the atmosphere and found the game scary. Your suggestion about the jumpscare is very helpful. I understand what you mean about the entity appearing directly in the player's face adding more variety to where it appears and making the encounter feel more unpredictable could definitely make it more terrifying.

Improving the ghost and scare moments is something I'm actively thinking about for future updates, so feedback like this helps a lot.

Thanks again for playing and sharing your thoughts!

reviews.review_title: Dev seems very active and nice
reviews.review_description: Had a few complaints and seems like the guy is already either fixing them or explained them, hyped to see where the game goes

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the honest feedback, really appreciate you taking the time to write this.

The fade to black when turning is actually intentional. The idea is to reflect the limitation of being in bed, you can’t just freely turn your head 180° in real life, so there’s a moment of disorientation built in. That said, I’ll keep an eye on how it feels in practice.

On the ghost difficulty: there is some leniency, but the core idea is that survival comes from actively following the rules and seeking out what’s happening  not just reacting. If you check the leaderboard, there are already players surviving 20+ minutes, so it’s definitely possible once it clicks.

About the party system, that was actually just added a few days ago 🙂 Previously it was only “play with friends.” I'm continuously improving multiplayer, so features like role preference are very much on the radar (or are you a fortune teller? 😄).

Appreciate the feedback again I'm actively working on the game and comments like this help guide what I improve next.

------

PS.: Literally, right now I'm releasing version 1.31.1, which will enable players to choose which role they prefer in Random multiplayer (Sleeper, Observer, No Preference).
I hope this makes your experience better :).

reviews.review_title: Too scared to keep my eyes open!
reviews.review_description: It’s amazing how I know that I am in my quiet safe home and that the bedroom and the ghosts in this game doesn’t really exist, but when I’m in the game it’s really scary! It’s so tempting to close your eyes when the door opens but you must resist and watch to see if a ghost enters and what that ghost does. If it looks at you, you must close your eyes to be safe. Then you have to judge when to open them again. Fun scarryness!

developer_response.body:
This is awesome to read, thank you!

That exact feeling is what I'm aiming for: knowing you’re safe, but still fighting the urge to close your eyes anyway 😄 The whole “look vs don’t look” tension is a big part of the experience, so it’s great to hear it’s landing the way I hoped.

You described the core loop perfectly too, observing carefully, reacting at the right moment, and taking that risk of when to open your eyes again.

Really glad you’re enjoying the “fun scaryness” and good luck resisting the urge to blink next time 👀

reviews.review_description: This is not scary at all  it looks like a screen overlay for the ghost.  It needs to be more immersive

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback! Sorry to hear SleepWatch didn’t scare you the way I hoped.

I was surprised by the 2-star rating, as many players are enjoying the experience and have left very positive reviews, but I understand VR horror can feel different for everyone. Immersion is very important to me, and I’m actively working on more scenes, better ghost animations, new behaviors, and more unpredictable events to make the game feel even more intense.

I appreciate you taking the time to share your thoughts, feedback like this helps me improve SleepWatch.

reviews.review_description: Just tried this for the 1st time tells you to close your eyes when she stares at you but look at her when she turns away but if you have your eyes closed how you supposed to know when she turns away? cant complain for 7p though

developer_response.body: That’s part of the gameplay :). You don’t know and that makes it more difficult:)

reviews.review_description: My wife got scared just from the sounds coming out of the headset. One of my friends threw off the VR headset in the first minute. As for me, I really loved the atmosphere, the sound design, and the truly terrifying vibe — plus the challenge level and overall quality are great. Highly recommended!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! 😄 Sounds like it did its job! You should definitely try the multiplayer mode, it lets you control what others see and makes scaring friends even more fun 👻

reviews.review_description:
Brilliant idea love it and really good to show friends and new people who wanna check VR out for the first time…

Imagine you could watch a horror film in the game lay on the bed with odd things moving around.. that would be an awesome idea.. 

Great work though to the developers

Cheers

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your kind review 😊
If you’d like to share it with your friends, there’s now a new multiplayer mode where you can control what they see in-game and scare them exactly the way you want 😄

reviews.review_description:
So download and switched on not sure what to expect. Got the first message saying "this game should be played laying down" so very intriguing. Legs came up and head went down and started a new game. Wow what a feeling it was, laying there, on a bed in a creepy room, listening, waiting and not knowing what to expect. Nearly had myself an accident after a grand total of 1 minute and 6 seconds....
Creepy little game which is great to show to people as you dont move or do anything other than click a button to close your eyes. Can't wait to scare the kids once they get back home.

developer_response.body: Ahahahaha! Make sure the kids will able to sleep after this kind of experience! :) Thanks for the review!

reviews.review_description:
Head twitching jump scares that will leave u contemplating sleep tonight and forever more. 

How long can you endure the terror that is sleep paralysis? 

Great horror survival with a heart pounding atmosphere that will keep your head on a swivel and the goosebumps rising, oh and not forgetting the evergrowing sweat from your brow. 

Certainly one to scare your friends - especially if they are new to VR 🤪

Make sure to play in a darkened room and lay on a bed for this one - it really adds to the effect 👌👍

developer_response.body: Love this description 😄👻 Thanks a lot! And you’re absolutely right, this one is perfect for scaring friends, especially in multiplayer mode where you can really mess with what they see 😈

reviews.review_description: Great Experince. Only thing you have to do is lay in your bed and choose to close your eyes or keep them opened. That's all. But makes this so very special. It just feels real. Biggest Immersion I've ever had.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much! 😊 That sense of immersion is exactly what I was aiming for. If you haven’t yet, try the multiplayer mode, it adds a fun new way to share (and amplify) the experience with friends 👻

reviews.review_description:
Well you won’t with this game, played laying on your bed or sitting on your couch, this truly is a horror game to scare the life out of you, you basically just look around the room and wait for the ghost to appear, close your eyes and hope it’s gone when you open them again…if not get ready, first time I nearly fell over in shock.
It’s a time challenge and can you get on the leaderboard, currently I can not stay focused enough.

Good little short horror game.

developer_response.body: Sweet dreams indeed 😄👻 Thanks for playing! If you’re brave enough, try the multiplayer mode, it adds a whole new way to mess with friends while they try to stay focused.

reviews.review_description: It really gives back the feeling of helplessness while certain moments can make you wet your pants.

developer_response.body: I really hope you can still sleep in the dark… but you might want to try the game again, now with multiplayer mode! 😄

reviews.review_description:
The game is great, and i would recommend it if you want wet pants.
However, if i start a run, it starts off normal. But, when the door closes, i see only the room, and when my controllers vibrate, i look around and i see nothing.
Then i die. This is pretty annoying and i have no idea if its supposed to be like this...

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the review and for the wet-pants warning 😄
We’re genuinely sorry that your experience led to a 3-star rating.

What you’re experiencing is actually part of the intended gameplay.
The controller vibration happens during thunder or lightning, even if nothing is visible at that exact moment. The core mechanic of the game is actively scanning the room and staying alert. The ghost doesn’t appear constantly, it can randomly manifest anywhere in the room and may either stare directly at you or appear while you’re looking away.

If the ghost appears and you don’t notice or react quickly enough, the run ends. So sometimes it can feel sudden, but that unpredictability is what creates the tension and fear.

That said, your feedback is valuable, if anything feels unclear or confusing, I’ll definitely look into improving how these mechanics are communicated in-game and hope I can change your mind on this. Thanks again for playing and sharing your experience!
